sn.s: Roberto me invita a cenar a su casa.
sn.e: Roberto invites me to dinner at his house.
"My mom wants to meet you," he says.

sn.s: Llego con un poco de nervios.

sn.e: "Come in, come in! Welcome," says his mom.
"Good evening, ma'am. Thank you for inviting me."

sn.s: La mesa está llena de comida.
sn.e: The table is full of food.

sn.s: "Hice gallo pinto y pollo. Coma, coma."
sn.e: "I made gallo pinto and chicken. Eat, eat."

sn.s: Me preguntan de mi familia, de mi país.
sn.e: They ask about my family, my country.

sn.s: "Aquí siempre tenés un plato en esta mesa," dice ella.
sn.e: "Here you always have a plate at this table," she says.
I feel like part of something.

sn.s: En Nicaragua, invitarte a comer es abrirte el corazón.
sn.e: In Nicaragua, inviting you to eat is opening their heart to you.
